name: Mimikatz
categoryDescription: Commonly abused tools that leave NO Windows registry artifacts. These tools are typically single executables, portable builds, or in-memory frameworks. Detection depends entirely on non-registry evidence: process execution logs, prefetch, ShimCache/Amcache, BAM/DAM, network traffic, and memory forensics. Includes detection notes for each tool.
description: Credential dumping tool extracting NTLM hashes, Kerberos tickets, and cleartext passwords from LSASS memory. Typically dropped as a standalone executable or run reflectively in memory. No installer, no registry entries (unless operator adds persistence separately).
forensic_value: No registry artifacts. WDigest registry key (HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\WDigest\UseLogonCredential=1) IS a registry IOC for Mimikatz preparation even though Mimikatz itself has no registry entries.
